recipient_and_agency_information.public_.answer: City of Riverbend Community Development Department

recipient_and_agency_information.address.answer: 350 Civic Center Plaza, Suite 200, Riverbend, CA 95827

project_identification.project_location_.answer: Northwest quadrant of the intersection of Harbor Boulevard and East Riverbend Parkway, Riverbend, Sacramento County, California 95827

project_identification.project_descripti.answer:
The Riverbend Station Mixed-Use Transit Village project proposes redevelopment of approximately 18.4 acres of underutilized light industrial and vacant parcels into a compact, pedestrian-oriented, mixed-use community adjacent to the existing Riverbend Light Rail Station in the City of Riverbend, Sacramento County, California. The project includes construction of up to 415 multi-family residential units in a mix of 4- to 6-story podium and wrap-style buildings, approximately 62,000 square feet of ground-floor neighborhood-serving commercial and office space, and a 1.8-acre publicly accessible central plaza that will function as a community gathering space and transit plaza.

The residential component includes a range of studio, one-bedroom, two-bedroom, and three-bedroom units, with 15 percent of the total units (approximately 62 units) restricted as affordable to low- and very low-income households for a minimum of 55 years. Amenities include rooftop open spaces, a fitness center, community rooms, bicycle storage rooms, and internal courtyards. The commercial component is anticipated to include small-format grocery, café/restaurant space, local retail, co-working office space, and service-oriented uses that support transit riders and neighborhood residents.

The project also includes associated infrastructure improvements such as internal private drives, new public and private utilities (water, wastewater, storm drainage, electricity, and telecommunications), enhanced pedestrian and bicycle facilities, a multi-use trail connection to the existing Riverbend Greenway along the American River, and streetscape improvements along Harbor Boulevard and East Riverbend Parkway. A structured parking garage of approximately 4.5 levels with up to 540 parking spaces will be constructed, along with limited surface parking areas for short-term and commercial use.

The project requires a General Plan Amendment to change the land use designation from Light Industrial to Transit-Oriented Mixed Use, a Zoning Map Amendment from M-1 (Light Industrial) to MU-TO (Mixed Use – Transit-Oriented), approval of a Planned Development Permit, Design Review approval, a Vesting Tentative Map to create multiple airspace lots and public easements, and related approvals and agreements. Construction is anticipated to occur in two main phases over approximately 36 to 42 months, with site preparation (including demolition of existing warehouse structures, grading, and installation of backbone infrastructure) commencing in mid-2027 and full buildout anticipated by late 2030, subject to market conditions.
